title: Age Defiance: Starting Over Later in Life

emotionalArc.struggle: Dream deferred - failed Cuba swim at 28, gave up swimming for 30 years, thought it was too late
emotionalArc.perseverance: Returned to swimming at 60, failed 4 more times, continued despite age and obstacles; started exercising at 56 after sister's death; went back to school at 65
emotionalArc.breakthrough: Completed Cuba swim at 64, became world champion bodybuilder, graduated college at 69

emotionalMoments.description: Failed Cuba swim at 28, gave up swimming completely
Realized at 60 she was no longer a doer, only watching others
Swimsuit moment with sister Rose - both laughed at how they looked
Sister Rose died suddenly of brain aneurysm - devastated, fell into depression
Got herself together, continued promise to Rose, started working out again
Completed Cuba swim at 64 - thousands of people crying on beach
Won first bodybuilding competition, became oldest female bodybuilder in world
Graduated college at 69 - surprise party, cap and gown, tears
Diploma on dresser, reminder that it's never too late

storyStructure.beginning: Failed swim at 28, gave up for 30 years
storyStructure.middle: Returned at 60, multiple failures; sister's death and recovery; starting exercise at 56; going back to school at 65
storyStructure.end: Triple triumph - swimming, bodybuilding, education - all achieved in 60s and 70s
